description: Wave 9 post-review FIX wave. Closes the blocking integration gaps and security/correctness findings from /code-review on the ralph/padrino-v9 branch. The Wave 9 modules (US-121..157) exist and are unit-tested but several core human-play seams were never wired into the production run path, and the anonymity-by-timing invariant is violable in the real path. These stories WIRE the existing machinery, harden the new human-facing surfaces, and strengthen the invariant CI gate so the gaps cannot regress. Runs on the existing ralph/padrino-v9 branch (continues the wave); after this completes and re-passes /code-review, the whole branch merges to main. Run with ralph/ralph-codex.sh (Codex / gpt-5.5).

userStories.title: Wire the human-play tick + HumanAdapter into the production executor
userStories.description: BLOCKER. src/padrino/runner/human_lane.py `_default_human_game_executor` runs the BENCHMARK runner `run_game(config, adapter, False)` with `NoopMockAdapter`; the human-play machinery `run_human_tick` (US-138) and `HumanAdapter` (US-137) have ZERO callers under src/. A launched human game therefore never consumes human-submitted actions through the intended seam and applies no symmetric release delay. Wire the real loop: the human lane must drive turns via `run_human_tick`, human seats served by a `HumanAdapter` bound to the action store, AI seats served by their normal curated adapter.
acceptanceCriteria: src/padrino/runner/human_lane.py drives human-lane games via run_human_tick (not run_game); human seats are served by HumanAdapter constructed from settings (deadline from padrino_human_phase_deadline_seconds) and reading the human action store; AI auto-fill seats keep their curated adapter.

An integration-style test launches a mixed human+AI game and shows a human action submitted via the POST action channel is actually consumed by the running game through HumanAdapter (no stub substitution).

A guard test asserts run_human_tick AND HumanAdapter each have at least one production (src/) caller, and that no human-lane game is executed via run_game/NoopMockAdapter; this test must fail if the executor regresses to the benchmark runner.
uv run ruff check . && uv run ruff format --check . && uv run mypy src tests && uv run pytest -m "not integration" all pass.
userStories.notes: Root-cause fix, not a bandaid: the executor seam (HumanGameExecutor/adapter_factory) already exists; supply the human tick + adapter through it rather than special-casing.

userStories.title: Apply symmetric release delay to human chat in the live path (close the timing leak)
userStories.description: BLOCKER (anonymity invariant). src/padrino/api/human_chat.py releases human chat synchronously at POST time (released_at=now), while AI public messages emit at tick resolution. The fixed symmetric release delay built for US-138 is never applied, so an observer can distinguish human vs AI by message timing before the endgame reveal. Route human public/private messages through the SAME release buffer/schedule as AI messages.
acceptanceCriteria: Human public/private messages are held and released on the same fixed release schedule as AI messages (the US-138 buffer), not at the instant of the POST.

A regression test asserts release-timing symmetry: a human message and an AI message submitted in the same phase surface to observers at the same buffered release point, with no per-source timing signal.
The behaviour holds on the wired production path from US-158 (not only in a unit harness).
All backend gates pass.
userStories.notes: This is the exact channel US-138 was meant to close; verify against the real runner path, not a stub.

userStories.title: Human chat enters the hash chain via content_ref so AI seats observe it
userStories.description: BLOCKER (functional). Human chat is written only to the sidecar; no core event carrying content_ref is appended, so AI observations (built from the event log) never include human messages and mixed human+AI discussion is broken. The content_ref seam (core/human_chat.human_chat_content_ref, the optional content_ref on PublicMessageSubmitted/PrivateMessageSubmitted) has no producer. Append the core event carrying ONLY the content_ref (sidecar keeps the raw text), preserving the out-of-band invariant and deterministic replay.
acceptanceCriteria: When a human message is released, a core PublicMessageSubmitted/PrivateMessageSubmitted event is appended carrying content_ref (hash) and NO raw text; the raw text remains only in the sidecar.
In a mixed game, an AI seat's observation includes the human's released public message (display surfaces resolve the text from the sidecar by ref; the chained event carries only the ref).
Deterministic replay is unchanged: chat events stay recorded-only and replay does not depend on chat text; hash-chain canonicalization is preserved (existing replay/round-trip tests still pass).
The out-of-band invariant test confirms no raw human text is ever placed in a hash-chained event.
All backend gates pass.
userStories.notes: Keep GDPR-erasure safe: redacting the sidecar must leave the chain valid.

userStories.title: Persist durable runtime snapshots in the tick loop + rehydrate on worker startup
userStories.description: BLOCKER (durability). runtime_repo.upsert (the HumanGameRuntime snapshot writer) has no production caller and rehydrate_active_human_games reads a stale/missing row, so a worker restart loses live games. Persist the snapshot each tick and rehydrate active human games on worker start.
acceptanceCriteria: The human tick loop upserts the HumanGameRuntime snapshot (phase, deadline_at, buffer) consistent with the event log on each tick / phase transition.
The human worker lane calls rehydrate_active_human_games on startup and resumes in-flight games at the correct phase/deadline without losing or duplicating a turn.
A restart-mid-phase regression test: write snapshot -> simulate process restart -> rehydrate -> the game resumes at the right phase with buffered submissions intact.
All backend gates pass.

userStories.title: Wire disconnect-grace + silent AI takeover into the worker lane
userStories.description: BLOCKER (US-150 unwired). take_over_seat / seats_past_grace have no production caller and no presence/heartbeats are recorded, so disconnect -> AI takeover never fires. Record seat presence, evaluate the grace window in the tick loop, and persist the takeover.
acceptanceCriteria: The worker lane records per-seat presence/heartbeats and, after the grace window, calls take_over_seat; the SeatTakenOver event is persisted in order with the tick loop and seat_kind flips to AI_TAKEOVER (occupant_principal_id retained for reveal provenance).
A reconnect within grace cancels the pending takeover; a reconnect racing an in-flight takeover neither double-applies nor loses the turn (regression test for the race).
Endgame reveal reports HUMAN_THEN_AI provenance for a taken-over seat.
All backend gates pass.
userStories.notes: ELO penalty hooks stay dormant (v1 casual).

userStories.title: Participant-reachable endgame reveal for private human games
userStories.description: BLOCKER (decision-11 unreachable). The only reveal route, /public/games/{id}/reveal, is gated on is_broadcastable && broadcast_state==RECENT; private/anonymous human games are never broadcastable, so participants can never get the endgame disclosure (who was human, roles, exact models). Add a participant-gated reveal route for terminal human games, reusing the single reveal projection.
acceptanceCriteria: A route (e.g. GET /human/games/{game_id}/reveal) returns project_endgame_reveal for a TERMINAL human game to an authenticated participant (a seat occupant of that game); it returns 403 for non-participants and 404/409 before the game is terminal.
It reuses the single core/reveal.project_endgame_reveal schema (no second reveal projection).
The frontend reveal page (US-156) consumes this route for human/lobby games; an e2e test covers a private ANONYMOUS game completing and a participant reaching its reveal.
No reveal is served before terminal; anonymity holds pre-terminal. All backend + frontend gates (pnpm lint/check/test:e2e) pass.
userStories.notes: This is the payoff of the imitation game; without it the spot-the-AI loop has no truth to reveal.

userStories.title: Harden OAuth identity assertion + session-bound state + expiry on upgrade
userStories.description: SECURITY. src/padrino/api/oauth.py trusts the userinfo sub/id with no id_token signature/aud/iss/nonce validation; the OAuth state cookie is SameSite=Lax and not bound to the in-flight guest session (login-CSRF / guest-absorption); _in_flight_guest_id upgrades expired (non-revoked) guest sessions, disagreeing with get_human_context.
acceptanceCriteria: When the provider returns an id_token, its signature, aud (== configured client_id), iss, and a nonce bound to the start request are validated before the identity is trusted; a tampered/missing/mismatched token rejects the sign-in.
The OAuth state/verifier is bound to the specific in-flight guest session; a callback whose state does not match the originating session is rejected.
_in_flight_guest_id rejects expired sessions (consistent with get_human_context); an expired guest session is never upgraded or merged.
Regression tests cover each path (tampered/missing id_token, mismatched/replayed state, expired guest). All backend gates pass.
userStories.notes: Real provider credentials remain a deploy-time step; validate the LOGIC against a stubbed signed token/JWKS, no live provider needed in tests.

userStories.title: Atomic cost-governance admission + correct day-boundary + AI_TAKEOVER spend
userStories.description: COST GOVERNANCE. src/padrino/economics/human_cost_governance.py admit_human is read-then-write with no lock, so concurrent create/join/launch requests all admit past the per-user/day caps and the breaker. Daily inference-$ is keyed to Game.created_at, so a game spanning UTC midnight escapes the daily cap. global_human_lane_spend_usd filters seat_kind=='HUMAN' only, excluding AI_TAKEOVER, undercounting spend.
acceptanceCriteria: Admission is atomic under concurrency (SELECT ... FOR UPDATE / reservation row / DB constraint) so N concurrent admissions cannot exceed the per-user/day caps or bypass the breaker; a concurrency regression test proves it.
Daily inference-$ is attributed to the spend/charge time, not Game.created_at, so a game spanning the day boundary is counted in the correct day.
global_human_lane_spend_usd counts the full human-lane seat set {HUMAN, AI_TAKEOVER} (consistent with human_lane/durability definitions).
All backend gates pass.
userStories.notes: Sized to the Moderate $500-$2k/mo envelope; the breaker must remain throttle-not-kill (never boots an active game).

userStories.title: Race-free human-chat sidecar sequence allocation
userStories.description: CORRECTNESS. src/padrino/db/repositories/human_chat_submissions.py next_sidecar_sequence is read-max-then-+1 with no lock; two concurrent human chats in the same game compute the same ordinal and collide on uq_human_chat_message_sequence (HTTP 500 / dropped message). Concurrent chat is the normal multiplayer case.
acceptanceCriteria: Sidecar sequence allocation is concurrency-safe (atomic per-game counter, or insert-with-retry on the unique violation) so concurrent submissions in the same game/phase all persist with distinct sequences.
A regression test simulating concurrent chat submissions asserts no IntegrityError surfaces to the client and no message is dropped.
All backend gates pass.
userStories.notes: Keep the (game_id, sequence) uniqueness; just make allocation safe.

userStories.title: Chat rate limiting fails closed + per-principal fairness
userStories.description: SECURITY/FAIRNESS. src/padrino/api/human_chat.py skips rate limiting entirely when the injected store is None (fail-open), and the per-game/phase ceiling is shared across all seats so one principal can exhaust the phase budget and 429 everyone else.
acceptanceCriteria: A missing/None rate-limit store fails CLOSED (deny, or a safe in-process default limiter) rather than silently allowing unlimited chat.
The per-game/phase budget is enforced with per-principal fairness so one seat flooding cannot block another seat's legitimate message in the same phase (regression test with two principals).
All backend gates pass.
userStories.notes: Security controls fail closed (mirrors the anonymity-guard convention).

userStories.title: Avoid full-log replay on every human request + rate-limit the action channel
userStories.description: PERFORMANCE. Each action POST, chat POST, and observation poll runs events_repo.list_events + replay_state_from_rows, re-sealing and re-folding the ENTIRE event log (O(events)). For the minutes-to-hours games this lane targets, latency grows with game length and eats the phase-deadline window. The action channel also has no dedicated rate limiter (only the shared session bucket).
acceptanceCriteria: Action POST, chat POST, and observation poll resolve current game state without re-folding the entire event log on every request (use the durable snapshot/cached head from US-161 plus bounded incremental reads).
The action POST channel has its own rate limiter distinct from the shared per-session read bucket.
A test asserts per-request state resolution does not grow O(events) for a long game (bounded number of new-event reads per request).
All backend gates pass.
userStories.notes: Correctness must not regress: the snapshot must stay consistent with the event log (US-161).

userStories.title: Strengthen the anonymity/segregation CI gate to catch production-wiring gaps
userStories.description: TEST HARDENING. The US-146 invariant gate passed while the production loop was unwired because it exercised projection/stubs, not the real run path. Extend the CI-enforced gate to assert real wiring so these gaps cannot silently regress.
acceptanceCriteria: The invariant test suite (run by default in pytest -m "not integration") asserts: (a) human and AI message release timing is symmetric on the wired path; (b) a released human chat message is observable by an AI seat via the event log; (c) a terminal private game's reveal is reachable by a participant; (d) the human-lane executor uses run_human_tick + HumanAdapter and no human game runs through run_game/NoopMockAdapter.
Each assertion fails if the corresponding wiring regresses (verify by a quick local mutation check noted in progress.txt).
All backend gates pass.
userStories.notes: Closes the systemic 'green via stubs' hole that let US-131/137/138/150 ship unwired.

userStories.title: Latent-bug + duplication cleanup from the review
userStories.description: CLEANUP. Lower-severity review findings: get_or_create_humans_included ignores the ruleset_id argument (returns the first humans-included league for every ruleset, cross-wiring rulesets); the seat-authorization helper _resolve_seat is copy-pasted across the 4 human routes (a future authz hardening could miss one); HumanGameRuntime.buffer_snapshot is NOT NULL with no server_default; turing _accuracy_string derives its string from a binary float.
acceptanceCriteria: get_or_create_humans_included filters by ruleset_id so each ruleset gets its own dormant humans-included league (regression test for two rulesets).
The human seat-authorization check (_resolve_seat) lives in one shared helper consumed by all human routes (action/chat/observation/turing).

HumanGameRuntime.buffer_snapshot has a server_default (or is documented ORM-only with a test asserting inserts supply it); add a migration if a server_default is introduced (linear, after the current head).
turing accuracy disclosure is derived without a binary-float repr artifact (exact ratio/decimal string).
All backend gates pass.
userStories.notes: Prefer wrapping the existing public.moderation guard adapter over duplicating it, but only if it does not risk behaviour drift; otherwise leave the two adapters and note it.
